goal: Attention queue resolve/defer mutations — backend endpoints + frontend wiring

acceptance_criteria.description: PATCH /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/attention/{itemId}/resolve endpoint updates exception disposition to RESOLVED with resolution_note and resolved_by from JWT
PATCH /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/attention/{itemId}/defer endpoint updates exception disposition to DEFERRED with resolution_note and resolved_by from JWT
Only PENDING items can be resolved or deferred — attempting on a non-PENDING item returns 409 Conflict
Both endpoints require auth middleware and editor-or-above role. Unauthorized returns 403.
WebSocket broadcasts attention_resolved and attention_deferred events to engagement members
Frontend AttentionQueue component wires resolve/defer buttons to API endpoints with optimistic update and error rollback

critical: PATCH endpoints use auth middleware — editor or owner role required
Disposition update is tenant-scoped via RLS on exception table
Only PENDING items can transition — enforce at DB query level, not just handler
resolved_by comes from JWT sub claim, not request body
high: WebSocket broadcasts attention_resolved/attention_deferred to engagement members
Frontend uses optimistic update with rollback on 4xx/5xx
Tests cover: resolve success, defer success, resolve non-pending (409), unauthorized (403), not-found (404)
Activity log event created for each resolve/defer action
medium: Bulk resolve/defer endpoint for multiple items in one request
